betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6ICF Reactor§f

§aThe second most powerful reactor — Inertial Confinement Fusion for massive energy and stellar flux!§f

The §bInertial Confinement Fusion Reactor§f (ICF) is a late-game fusion reactor that uses two modular laser emitters focused onto a small fuel pellet. Besides the DFC, it is the most powerful energy-producing device in NTM, capable of multiple GHE/s.

§6Reactor Chamber§f:
The primary reaction chamber has a GUI with the following indicators:
§b1.§f Thermal Output — total laser input in TU
§b2.§f Cold Cooling Fluid Buffer — up to §e512,000 mB§f
§b3.§f Fluid Identifier — selects coolant type
§b4.§f Pellet Input — accepts fuel pellets
§b5.§f Current Pellet — undergoing fusion
§b6.§f Pellet Output — depleted pellets
§b7.§f Hot Cooling Fluid Buffer
§b8.§f Reactor Heat — up to §e1,000,000,000,000 TU§f
§b9.§f Stellar Flux — up to §e24,000 mB§f

§6Fuel Pellets§f:
Made in the §bICF Fuel Pellet Maker§f (plasma forge). Each pellet contains §e1,000 mB§f of each fluid, making fuel production highly efficient. Pellets can be §bmuon catalyzed§f to reduce startup temperature.

Depleted pellets yield: Empty Pellet Housing + Ionized Particles + Iron Powder.

§6Operation§f:
§b• ICF cannot melt down — the only hazard is laser line-of-sight
§b• Variable laser power allows tuning output
§b• Produces §bStellar Flux§f as a byproduct (used for Heavy Duty Elements)

§6Pros§f:
§b• Cannot melt down
§b• Immense power output
§b• Variable/tunable output
§b• Cheap, simple fuel
§b• Produces stellar flux passively

§6Cons§f:
§b• Immensely expensive to build
§b• Absolutely massive (wider than a chunk lengthwise)
§b• High startup power requirement

§eTip§f: The ICF is your stepping stone to the DFC. Use it to generate stellar flux for heavy duty elements, which are needed for Gerald. Without the ICF, you cannot produce the materials needed for the DFC!
